title: Designing for Failure
concept: Assuming agent steps will fail, and building retry, fallback, and escalation paths into the graph.

exercises.prompt: What is important to consider when creating an AI agent?

options.text: Planning for steps that may fail

exercises.explain: It's crucial to plan for possible failures to make AI agents reliable.

cards.title: Hook: Preparing for the Unexpected
cards.body: In designing AI agents, we must expect that things can go wrong. Creating solutions for failure helps us avoid major issues.

cards.title: Definition: Designing for Failure
cards.body: Designing for failure means creating backup plans in case something goes wrong. This can include retries, fallbacks, and escalation paths for the agent.

cards.title: Analogy: Mountain Climbing
cards.body: Just like a climber carries ropes and gear to recover from slips, AI agents need paths to recover from failures, ensuring they can keep going.

cards.title: Example: Retry Mechanism
cards.body: If an AI agent fails to get a response from a server, it should automatically try again before giving up or seeking alternatives.

cards.title: Myth: Failing Means You Didn’t Succeed
cards.body: It's a myth that planning for failure implies weak design. It actually makes your systems stronger and more reliable!

cards.title: Visual: Success vs. Failure Paths
cards.body: Designing for failure adds extra pathways for success. Without these, failure leads to dead ends.

left.text: One path, leads to a stop if it fails.

right.text: Multiple paths, allowing retries and fallbacks.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a strategy for designing AI agents?

options.text: Making everything perfect from the start

exercises.explain: Building retry mechanisms is a smart way to handle potential failures, ensuring agents can try again instead of giving up.

exercises.prompt: Designing for failure means disregarding potential mistakes.
exercises.explain: It's false! Designing for failure means preparing for mistakes to create more robust AI agents.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence: Designing for failure involves creating _____ pathways.
exercises.sentence: Designing for failure involves creating fallback pathways.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'fallback' as it directly refers to the backup paths in the design.

exercises.prompt: Which step is NOT part of designing for failure?
exercises.explain: Dismissing error scenarios is NOT part of designing for failure. In fact, considering them is critical for robust agent design.

exercises.prompt: What is a reliable approach when building AI agents for unexpected failures?

options.text: Ignore potential errors and focus on success
Assume every action will succeed every time
Stop all actions at the first sign of issues

exercises.explain: The best approach is to add retry and fallback methods. This prepares the agent to handle failures effectively and stay functional.
